Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Represents a physical asset such as equipment, structure, or component that needs inspection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the asset.

location.description: The physical location of the asset.

qrCode.description: The QR code associated with the asset for easy identification.

documentIds.description: References to Documents associated with the asset. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Document)

AssetType.title: AssetType
AssetType.description: Represents a template for different types of assets (e.g., tanks, vessels, piping).

id.description: Unique identifier for the AssetType entity.

name.description: The name of the asset type (e.g., Tank, Vessel).

description.description: A detailed description of the asset type.

templateData.description: JSON data defining the specific attributes or configurations for this asset type.

Inspection.title: Inspection
Inspection.description: Represents an inspection event performed on an asset.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

scheduleDate.description: The date the inspection is scheduled to occur.

completionDate.description: The date the inspection was completed.

status.description: The current status of the inspection (e.g., Scheduled, In Progress, Completed).

reportId.description: Reference to the InspectionReport generated for this inspection. (Relationship: Inspection 1:1 InspectionReport)

InspectionReport.title: InspectionReport
InspectionReport.description: Represents the report generated after an inspection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InspectionReport entity.

reportData.description: The actual report data, likely stored as a JSON string or similar format.

approvalLevel3Id.description: Reference to User (Level III) who approved the report.

defectIds.description: References to Defect records found during the inspection. (Relationship: InspectionReport 1:N Defect)

documentIds.description: References to Supporting Documents for the Inspection Report. (Relationship: InspectionReport 1:N Document)

Defect.title: Defect
Defect.description: Represents a defect identified during an inspection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Defect entity.

type.description: The type of the defect (e.g., crack, corrosion).

location.description: The location of the defect on the asset.

photoUrls.description: URLs of photos documenting the defect.

videoUrls.description: URLs of videos documenting the defect.

waveformUrl.description: URL of waveform data associated with the defect.

JobRequest.title: JobRequest
JobRequest.description: Represents a job request created by a client for NDT inspection services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobRequest entity.

location.description: The location where the job needs to be performed.

technique.description: The NDT technique required for the job (e.g., UT, RT).

standards.description: The industry standards to be followed during the inspection.

urgency.description: The urgency level of the job request.

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset to be inspected. (Relationship: Asset 1:N JobRequest)

Bid.title: Bid
Bid.description: Represents a bid submitted by an inspector for a job request.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bid entity.

price.description: The price quoted in the bid.

availability.description: The inspector's availability to perform the job.

certifications.description: Certifications held by the inspector relevant to the job.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document (e.g., drawing, photo, certificate).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

url.description: The URL where the document is stored.

type.description: The type of document (e.g., Drawing, Photo, Certificate).

uploadDate.description: The date the document was uploaded.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user (e.g., Client, Inspector, Admin).

company.description: The company the user works for.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  User ID is used for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: assetTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the asset type.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the asset.

definition.description: Stores information about inspections performed on assets.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the asset.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the inspection.

definition.description: Stores inspection reports associated with inspections.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the inspection.

params.name: inspectionReportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the inspection report.

definition.description: Stores information about defects found during inspections.

params.name: inspectionReportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the inspection report.

params.name: defectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the defect.

definition.description: Stores job requests created by users. Path-based ownership is used for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (client).

params.name: jobRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the job request.

definition.description: Stores bids submitted for job requests.

params.name: jobRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the job request.

params.name: bidId
params.description: The unique identifier of the bid.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the NDT Exchange application, focusing on asset management, job marketplace, and inspection workflows. The structure emphasizes authorization independence, clarity, and scalability.  Path-based ownership is used for user-specific data (e.g., JobRequests created by a Client) while membership maps are utilized for collaborative data (not explicitly defined in this data model).  Denormalization is applied where necessary to avoid complex security rules and ensure atomic operations.

**Authorization Independence:** The design uses path-based ownership where appropriate. For example, JobRequests are stored under `/users/{userId}/job_requests/{jobRequestId}`, directly associating them with the user who created them (clientId). This denormalizes the ownership and eliminates the need for `get()` calls to verify the user's identity.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Segregation ensures secure list operations.  Storing JobRequests under `/users/{userId}/job_requests/{jobRequestId}` allows clients to list only their job requests.  Similarly, storing Bids under `/job_requests/{jobRequestId}/bids/{bidId}` allows Inspectors to list bids only for specific job requests. This segregation, combined with appropriate security rules, ensures that users can only access the data they are authorized to see, enabling QAPs.
